data_summary.scoring_mode_rationale: Hotspot scoring uses ALL validation statuses. Deliberate decision: approved + pending/processing rows both represent real parking events observed in the field. Rerun with --approved-only to cross-check rankings.

point_level_dbscan.role: Stage A: labels individual violation records. High cluster count (733) is expected and correct — each cluster represents one block-level parking pocket (~100 m radius). These fine-grained clusters are inputs to the junction EDA chart and per-record JSON output. They are NOT aggregated for silhouette because H3 cell grouping in Stage B collapses them into the neighbourhood-level clusters that are actually patrol-actionable.

centroid_level_dbscan.silhouette_interpretation: Stage B: computed on H3 cell centroids (city-block scale). Values > 0.5 indicate strong neighbourhood grouping — hotspots form real geographic clusters, not random scatter across the city. Values near 0 indicate overlapping zones (expected in a dense city).
centroid_level_dbscan.note: Centroid-level DBSCAN (eps ~550 m) found 137 city-block-scale clusters from 776 H3 cell centroids. Silhouette = 0.6983 (range -1 to 1; >0.5 = strong structure).

dbscan_validation.coordinate_distortion_note: DBSCAN uses raw decimal-degree (lat, lon) Euclidean distance. At Bengaluru's latitude (~12.9°N) 1° longitude ≈ 97 km vs 1° latitude ≈ 111 km — ~14% east-west compression. The eps thresholds are calibrated against latitude distances; east-west cluster radii are ~14% smaller than stated. Upgrade to DBSCAN(metric='haversine') with radian coords for production.
dbscan_validation.note: Two-stage DBSCAN (PRD-1): Stage A (point-level, ~100 m) labels 733 micro-clusters from 298,445 records — each cluster is one local parking pocket. Stage B (centroid-level, ~550 m) groups 776 H3 cells into 137 patrol-zone neighbourhoods. Silhouette 0.6983 on Stage B confirms real geographic structure.

eps_weight_rationale: Enforcement Priority Score (EPS) = 0.35×violation_volume + 0.20×daily_persistence + 0.15×peak_hour_concentration + 0.20×junction_proximity + 0.10×repeat_offender_rate. Weights are heuristic; calibrate against real traffic-speed data. IMPORTANT — tier labels (CRITICAL/HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW) are dataset-relative percentile assignments: CRITICAL = top 3%, HIGH = top 15%, MEDIUM = top 40%, LOW = remainder. These thresholds shift with dataset composition changes.
hotspot_deduplication_note: FIX #10: Adjacent H3 cells sharing the same junction_name are merged into a single presentation record (highest EPS cell representative, summed violation counts). Full per-cell data is in hotspot_summary.csv. This eliminates duplicate entries like 'Sagar Theatre Junction' appearing at ranks 4 and 5.

problem_statement_answer.detect: H3 hex-grid aggregation + two-stage DBSCAN + 5-factor EPS score identified 24 CRITICAL and 95 HIGH-priority enforcement zones.
problem_statement_answer.quantify: Proxy congestion impact index (violations × junction-proximity weight × peak-hour multiplier) shows the top-10 hotspots account for 37.8% of total citywide violation-induced flow disruption.
problem_statement_answer.enable_targeted_enforcement: CP-SAT zone-aware route optimization assigns officers to the highest-priority cells within bounded, deployable patrol routes (see officer_deployment.json).

eps_sensitivity_analysis.interpretation: Rank correlation close to 1.0 means top-10 hotspots are stable across weight schemes. Values < 0.90 indicate sensitivity to weight choices and warrant recalibration with real traffic-speed data.

repeat_offender_data_disclaimer: 100.0% of vehicle numbers in this dataset are anonymized/placeholder plates (e.g. 'FKN00GL0000'-style codes), not real license plates. Repeat-offender vehicle IDs below are shown for METHODOLOGY DEMONSTRATION ONLY and are NOT actionable plate numbers. This analysis becomes fully actionable once integrated with real, non-anonymized plate data from the traffic enforcement system.
